# k-cluster tips: 
# For final project: maybe do several random initializations for k-means, choose the one that has less final cost
# Or variance-based split, where if variance is too high, use less clusters

# Random seed: choose k values from set and put into k clusters. loop through whole set and put into each that is 
# closest to the k value "seed"

# silhouette score: balance important, maximize average silhouette coefficient, minimize number of peaks lower than avg
# silhouette coefficient
In [3]:
# Scale all numerical data, change all boolean to binary, vectorize text data
# while ensuring all sizes are consistent before hstack'ing

# Scale training data and test data the same way
def scaleDat(X,vectorizer,y,featureNames,j,X_test):

    ### 'verified': boolean to binary and check feature_score value
    df1 = csr_matrix(X["verified"].astype(int).to_numpy().reshape(-1,1))
    df1_test = csr_matrix(X_test["verified"].astype(int).to_numpy().reshape(-1,1))

    ### 'image': nan (->0) or picture (->1) to binary
    df2 = csr_matrix(np.array(X['image'].fillna(0).astype(bool).astype(int)).reshape(-1,1))
    df2_test = csr_matrix(np.array(X_test['image'].fillna(0).astype(bool).astype(int)).reshape(-1,1))
    
    # 'vote': nan -> 0 votes
    X['vote'].fillna(0, inplace=True)
    X_test['vote'].fillna(0, inplace=True)
    
    ### Scale numerical.. mean = 0, var = 1
    scaler = StandardScaler()
    
    # 'unixReviewTime': numerically scaled
    df3 = csr_matrix(scaler.fit_transform(X["unixReviewTime"].values.reshape(-1, 1)))
    df3_test = csr_matrix(scaler.transform(X_test["unixReviewTime"].values.reshape(-1, 1)))
    
    # 'vote': numerically scaled
    df4 = csr_matrix(scaler.fit_transform(X["vote"].values.reshape(-1, 1)))
    df4_test = csr_matrix(scaler.transform(X_test["vote"].values.reshape(-1, 1)))
    
    # 'reviewTime': string->numerical->scaled
    hold = X["reviewTime"].values.reshape(-1, 1)
    hold_t = X_test["reviewTime"].values.reshape(-1, 1)

    date_format = "%m %d, %Y"
    time_stamp = np.zeros((hold.shape[0],1))
    for t in range(hold.shape[0]):
        date_object = datetime.datetime.strptime(hold[t][0], date_format)
        time_stamp[t] = time.mktime(date_object.timetuple())
        
    time_stamp_t = np.zeros((hold_t.shape[0],1))
    for t in range(hold_t.shape[0]):
        date_object_t = datetime.datetime.strptime(hold_t[t][0], date_format)
        time_stamp_t[t] = time.mktime(date_object_t.timetuple())
        
    df5 = csr_matrix(scaler.fit_transform(time_stamp))
    df5_test = csr_matrix(scaler.transform(time_stamp_t))
    
    ### vectorize text features.. put all together into one feature
    
    # Fill in rest of NaN with an empty string
    X = X.fillna("")
    X_test = X_test.fillna("")
    
    # Having trouble to efficiently do this string->dic->string->vectorize for this feature.. but here is a way
    strDF = X["style"].fillna("")
    for e in range(X["style"].shape[0]):
        
        # If entry isnt blank (wasn't nan before), convert str->dic->str
        if strDF[e] != "":
            strDF[e] = " ".join([f"{k} {v}" for k, v in ast.literal_eval(strDF[e]).items()])
            
    strDF_test = X_test["style"].fillna("")
    for e in range(X_test["style"].shape[0]):
        
        # If entry isnt blank (wasn't nan before), convert str->dic->str
        if strDF_test[e] != "":
            strDF_test[e] = " ".join([f"{k} {v}" for k, v in ast.literal_eval(strDF_test[e]).items()])
    
    
    # Concatenate all text features into one feature
    comboText = X['reviewerID'] + " " + X['reviewerName']+ " " + X['asin'] + " " + X['reviewText'] + " " + X['summary'] + " " + X['category'] + " " + strDF
    comboText_test = X_test['reviewerID'] + " " + X_test['reviewerName']+ " " + X_test['asin'] + " " + X_test['reviewText'] + " " + X_test['summary'] + " " + X_test['category'] + " " + strDF_test
    
    df6 = vectorizer.fit_transform(comboText)
    df6_test = vectorizer.transform(comboText_test)
    
    # Stack the features
    df = hstack([df1,df2,df3,df4,df5,df6])
    df_test = hstack([df1_test,df2_test,df3_test,df4_test,df5_test,df6_test])
    
    ###########################################################################################################
    # While I'm here, check feature importance by leave one out. 
    # Can't loop this easily bc of text combination and data shapes that I originally chose
    # I don't have time to make this prettier :(
    if j == 1:
        f=0
        dfOut = hstack([df2,df3,df4,df5,df6])
        feature_score(dfOut,df,y,featureNames[f])

        f=f+1
        dfOut = hstack([df1,df3,df4,df5,df6])
        feature_score(dfOut,df,y,featureNames[f])

        f=f+1
        dfOut = hstack([df1,df2,df4,df5,df6])
        feature_score(dfOut,df,y,featureNames[f])

        f=f+1
        dfOut = hstack([df1,df2,df3,df5,df6])
        feature_score(dfOut,df,y,featureNames[f])

        f=f+1
        dfOut = hstack([df1,df2,df3,df4,df6])
        feature_score(dfOut,df,y,featureNames[f])

        f=f+1
        comboTextN = X['reviewerName']+ " " + X['asin'] + " " + X['reviewText'] + " " + X['summary'] + " " + X['category'] + " " + strDF
        dfN = vectorizer.fit_transform(comboTextN)
        dfOut = hstack([df1,df2,df3,df4,df5,dfN])
        feature_score(dfOut,df,y,featureNames[f])

        f=f+1
        comboTextN = X['reviewerID'] +  " " + X['asin'] + " " + X['reviewText'] + " " + X['summary'] + " " + X['category'] + " " + strDF
        dfN = vectorizer.fit_transform(comboTextN)
        dfOut = hstack([df1,df2,df3,df4,df5,dfN])
        feature_score(dfOut,df,y,featureNames[f])

        f=f+1
        comboTextN = X['reviewerID'] +  " " + X['reviewerName']+ " " + X['reviewText'] + " " + X['summary'] + " " + X['category'] + " " + strDF
        dfN = vectorizer.fit_transform(comboTextN)
        dfOut = hstack([df1,df2,df3,df4,df5,dfN])
        feature_score(dfOut,df,y,featureNames[f])

        f=f+1
        comboTextN = X['reviewerID'] +  " " + X['reviewerName']+ " " + X['asin'] + " " + X['summary'] + " " + X['category'] + " " + strDF
        dfN = vectorizer.fit_transform(comboTextN)
        dfOut = hstack([df1,df2,df3,df4,df5,dfN])
        feature_score(dfOut,df,y,featureNames[f])

        f=f+1
        comboTextN = X['reviewerID'] +  " " + X['reviewerName']+ " " + X['asin'] + " " + X['reviewText'] + " " + X['category'] + " " + strDF
        dfN = vectorizer.fit_transform(comboTextN)
        dfOut = hstack([df1,df2,df3,df4,df5,dfN])
        feature_score(dfOut,df,y,featureNames[f])

        f=f+1
        comboTextN = X['reviewerID'] +  " " + X['reviewerName']+ " " + X['asin'] + " " + X['reviewText'] + " " + X['summary'] + " " + strDF
        dfN = vectorizer.fit_transform(comboTextN)
        dfOut = hstack([df1,df2,df3,df4,df5,dfN])
        feature_score(dfOut,df,y,featureNames[f])

        f=f+1
        comboTextN = X['reviewerID'] +  " " + X['reviewerName']+ " " + X['asin'] + " " + X['reviewText'] + " " + X['summary'] + " " + X['category']
        dfN = vectorizer.fit_transform(comboTextN)
        dfOut = hstack([df1,df2,df3,df4,df5,dfN])
        feature_score(dfOut,df,y,featureNames[f])
    
    return df,strDF,df_test
In [4]:
def binaryCutoff(y,cutoff):
    yb = np.zeros((y.shape[0],cutoff.shape[0]))
    for c in range(cutoff.shape[0]):
        yb[:,c] = (y > cutoff[c]).astype(int).to_numpy()
    return yb
In [5]:
# See if any features dont add any value to model
def feature_score(df1,df2,y,featureName):
    k_best1= SelectKBest(score_func=f_classif, k="all")
    X1 = k_best1.fit_transform(df1, y)
    
    k_best2= SelectKBest(score_func=f_classif, k="all")
    X2 = k_best2.fit_transform(df2, y)

    # Get F-scores and P-values
    feature_scores1 = k_best1.scores_  # ANOVA F-values
    feature_pvalues1 = k_best1.pvalues_  # Corresponding p-values
    
    feature_scores2 = k_best2.scores_  # ANOVA F-values
    feature_pvalues2 = k_best2.pvalues_  # Corresponding p-values

    threshf = 1
    threshp = 0.1
    
    delt_feat = len(feature_scores2[(feature_scores2>threshf) & (feature_pvalues2<threshp)]) - len(feature_scores1[(feature_scores1>threshf) & (feature_pvalues1<threshp)])
    perc_delt_feat = (-delt_feat/len(feature_scores1))*100
    perc_feat = (delt_feat/(df2.shape[1]-df1.shape[1]))*100
    f_lost = (np.sum(k_best2.scores_)-np.sum(k_best1.scores_))/delt_feat
    
    print("If we remove ","-",featureName,"-",":")
    print("Δ features in final df with F-score > ", threshf, " and  p-value < ", threshp, ": ", delt_feat)
    print("If above line are 'good' features, (Good feature lost)/(total features lost), if I remove this feature:", perc_feat,"%")
    print("Total score loss per 'good' feature lost: " ,f_lost)
    print("")
    print("")
    print("---------------------------------------------------------------------------")
    print("")
    print("")

1. Binary Classification¶

In [9]:
def hyperTest(X_train_sub,y_train_sub,y_val,X_val,param_grid,model,c,test,X_train,y_train):
    
    # Perform Grid Search with Cross-Validation
    grid_search = GridSearchCV(model, param_grid, cv=5, scoring='accuracy', n_jobs=-1)
    grid_search.fit(X_train_sub, y_train_sub)

    # Get best model from grid search
    best_model = grid_search.best_estimator_
    print("cutoff = ", c)
    print("Best Hyperparameters:", grid_search.best_params_)
    
    # Perform 5-fold cross-validation on training set
    cv_scores = cross_val_score(best_model, X_train_sub, y_train_sub, cv=5, scoring='accuracy')
    print("Cross-Validation Accuracies on Training Set:", cv_scores)
    print("Mean CV Accuracy on Training Set:", round(cv_scores.mean(),3))
    
    # Train on best model
    best_model.fit(X_train, y_train)
    
    # Predict on validation set, which model has not seen
    y_val_pred = best_model.predict(X_val)

    # Compute Accuracy
    accuracy = accuracy_score(y_val, y_val_pred)
    print("Accuracy on Validation Set:", str(round(accuracy,3)))

    # Compute Confusion Matrix
    cm = confusion_matrix(y_val, y_val_pred)
    print("Confusion Matrix on Validation Set:\n", cm)

    # Compute Macro F1 Score
    macro_f1 = f1_score(y_val, y_val_pred, average='macro')
    print("Macro F1 Score on Validation Set:", str(round(macro_f1,3)))
    
    # Get raw scores for ROC
    if test == 1:
        y_val_prob = best_model.decision_function(X_val)
    else:
        y_val_prob = grid_search.predict_proba(X_val)
        y_val_prob = y_val_prob[:,1]
        
    # Compute ROC Curve
    fpr, tpr, _ = roc_curve(y_val, y_val_prob)
    roc_auc = roc_auc_score(y_val, y_val_prob)

    # Plot ROC Curve
    plt.plot(fpr, tpr, label=f'ROC curve (AUC = {roc_auc:.2f})')
    plt.plot([0, 1], [0, 1], 'k--')  # Diagonal reference line
    plt.xlabel("False Positive Rate")
    plt.ylabel("True Positive Rate")
    plt.title("ROC Curve on Validation Set")
    plt.legend()
    plt.show()

    print("AUC Score on Validation Set:", str(round(roc_auc,3)))
    print("")
    print("")
    print("---------------------------------------------------------------------------")
    print("")
    print("")
    
    return best_model
In [23]:
# Binary thresholds for labels
cutoff = np.array([1,2,3,4])
yb = binaryCutoff(y,cutoff)

# Split off a validation set
X_train, X_val, y_train, y_val = train_test_split(Xs, yb, test_size=0.2, random_state=82)

X_train_selected=[]
X_test_selected =[]
X_val_selected =[]
Xs_selected =[]
selector = SelectKBest(f_classif, k=1700)  # Select top features
#selector = SelectKBest(f_classif, k=5000)  # Select top features
for s in range(4):
    X_train_selected.append(selector.fit_transform(X_train, y_train[:,s]))
    X_test_selected.append(selector.transform(df_test))
    X_val_selected.append(selector.transform(X_val))
    #Xs_selected.append(selector.transform(Xs))

# Subset of training for hyperparameter tuning
# X_train_sub, _, y_train_sub, _ = train_test_split(X_train_selected, y_train, test_size=0.95, random_state=82)

a. Linear SVC¶

In [11]:
model1 = []
for c in range(y_train.shape[1]):
    
    # Define parameter grid for hyperparameter tuning
    param_grid = {
        'penalty': ['l1', 'l2'], # norm used in penalty
        'C': [0.01, 0.1, 1, 10, 100],  # Regularization strength
        'tol': [1e-7, 1e-5,1e-3,0.01],  # Convergence tolerance
        'loss': ['squared_hinge'],  # Loss function-> 'hinge' doesn't work
        'dual': [False],  #  n_samples > n_features, so set to false
        'class_weight': [None, 'balanced'],
        'max_iter': [5000]
    }

    # output the best parameters and their accuracy score for each cutoff
    
    #model1.append(hyperTest(X_train_sub,y_train_sub[:,c],y_val[:,c],X_val,param_grid,LinearSVC(),c+1,1,X_train,y_train[:,c]))
    
    # subset of training data for hyperparameter tuning
    X_train_sub, _, y_train_sub, _ = train_test_split(X_train_selected[c], y_train[:,c], test_size=0.9, random_state=82)
    
    model1.append(hyperTest(X_train_sub,y_train_sub,y_val[:,c],X_val_selected[c],param_grid,LinearSVC(),c+1,1,X_train_selected[c],y_train[:,c]))

In [12]:
model2=[]
for c in range(y_train.shape[1]):
    
    # Define parameter grid for hyperparameter tuning
    param_grid = {
        'C': [1, 10, 100],  # Regularization parameter
        'kernel': ['rbf','poly'],  # Kernel types
        'tol': [1e-7, 1e-5, 1e-3, 1e-1],  # Convergence tolerance
    }

    # output the best parameters and their accuracy score for each cutoff
    #model2.append(hyperTest(X_train_sub,y_train_sub[:,c],y_val[:,c],X_val,param_grid,SVC(),c+1,1,X_train,y_train[:,c]))
    X_train_sub, _, y_train_sub, _ = train_test_split(X_train_selected[c], y_train[:,c], test_size=0.95, random_state=82)
    
    model2.append(hyperTest(X_train_sub,y_train_sub,y_val[:,c],X_val_selected[c],param_grid,SVC(),c+1,1,X_train_selected[c],y_train[:,c]))

for c in range(y_train.shape[1]):
    
    # Define parameter grid for hyperparameter tuning
    param_grid = {
        'penalty': [None,'l1', 'l2', 'elasticnet'],  # Regularization types
        'C': [0.1,1,10,50,100,200],  # Regularization strength
        'solver': ['liblinear', 'lbfgs', 'newton-cg', 'newton-cholesky', 'sag'],  # Solver algorithms
        'max_iter': [500],
        'class_weight': [None,'balanced']
    }
    
    # subset of training data for hyperparameter tuning
    X_train_sub, _, y_train_sub, _ = train_test_split(X_train_selected[c], y_train[:,c], test_size=0.95, random_state=82)
    
    # output the best parameters and their accuracy score for each cutoff
    model3.append(hyperTest(X_train_sub,y_train_sub,y_val[:,c],X_val_selected[c],param_grid,LogisticRegression(),c+1,1,X_train_selected[c],y_train[:,c]))

    param_grid = {
         'hidden_layer_sizes': [100],  # Number of neurons in each hidden layer

g. AdaBoost¶

Performance not great.. stopped partway through -> f1 = 0.588 for cutoff 1. No need to run the rest¶

In [ ]:
model7=[]
for c in range(y_train.shape[1]):
    
    # Define parameter grid for hyperparameter tuning
    param_grid = {
        'n_estimators': [20,50],  # Number of boosting stages
        'learning_rate': [0.01, 0.1, 1.0],  # Step size
        'base_estimator': [None],  # Use the default weak estimator (decision trees)
    }

    # output the best parameters and their accuracy score for each cutoff
    X_train_sub, _, y_train_sub, _ = train_test_split(X_train_selected[c], y_train[:,c], test_size=0.95, random_state=82)
    
    model7.append(hyperTest(X_train_sub,y_train_sub[:,c],y_val[:,c],X_val,param_grid,AdaBoostClassifier(),c+1,0,X_train,y_train[:,c]))
    # f1 score -> ~0.77

Output best model to Kaggle for Binary¶

Previous try¶

In [ ]:
# Get probability data of models

from sklearn.calibration import CalibratedClassifierCV
mod1p=[]
mod3p=[]
for s in range(4):
    calibrated_model1 = CalibratedClassifierCV(model1[s], method='sigmoid', cv=5)  # Apply probability calibration
    mod1p.append(calibrated_model1.fit(Xs, yb[:,s]))

    calibrated_model3 = CalibratedClassifierCV(model3[s], method='sigmoid', cv=5)  # Apply probability calibration
    mod3p.append(calibrated_model3.fit(Xs, yb[:,s]))
In [ ]:
# TODO: train an ML model here using train
# use all data, including validation

for s in range(4):
    
    # Fit models to all training and test data, including validation
    #model1[s].fit(Xs,yb[:,s])
    #model3[s].fit(Xs,yb[:,s])
    #model2[s].fit(Xs,yb[:,s])
    

    # predict the label of the test samples here using the best model:
    # Linear SVM, NN, logistic, non-linear SVM all gave similar performance. NB only ok
    
    check_lsvm = mod1p[s].predict_proba(df_test)
    check_log = mod3p[s].predict_proba(df_test)
    #check_nb = model4[s].predict_proba(df_test)
    check_nn = model6[s].predict_proba(df_test)
    
    #test_predicted_all =  np.hstack([model1[s].predict(df_test).reshape(-1,1),model3[s].predict(df_test).reshape(-1,1),model4[s].predict(df_test).reshape(-1,1),model6[s].predict(df_test).reshape(-1,1)])
    test_predicted_all =  np.hstack([model1[s].predict(df_test).reshape(-1,1),model3[s].predict(df_test).reshape(-1,1),model6[s].predict(df_test).reshape(-1,1)])

    # F-stats for cutoff=3
    scaledF = np.array([0.78,0.791,0.788])
    
    # relative to guess
    scaledF = scaledF-0.5
    
    # each models' confidence for each data
    #prob = np.hstack([check_lsvm[:,1].reshape(-1,1),check_log[:,1].reshape(-1,1),check_nb[:,1].reshape(-1,1),check_nn[:,1].reshape(-1,1)])
    prob = np.hstack([check_lsvm[:,1].reshape(-1,1),check_log[:,1].reshape(-1,1),check_nn[:,1].reshape(-1,1)])

    # take into overall model reliability and its confidence in each data
    scaledF = scaledF*prob
    
    # scale to 1
    for d in range(scaledF.shape[0]):
        scaledF[d,:] = scaledF[d,:]/np.sum(scaledF[d,:],axis=0)

    scaledAns = test_predicted_all*scaledF
    
    allMod = np.sum(scaledAns,axis=1)
    allMod[allMod>0.5] = 1
    allMod[allMod<=0.5] = 0

    # A structure exists in the test data... Should randomize rows next time. Best I could do was around 80% 
    # with this method, per cutoff
    #test_predicted = median_filter(test_predicted, size=20, mode='nearest')

    # convert to int
    test_predicted = [int(x) for x in allMod]

    # IMPORTANT: use 'id' and 'binary_split_' as the column names
    test_ids = list(test.index) # the 'id' column name is the index of the test samples

    test_submission = pd.DataFrame({'id':test_ids, 'binary_split_'+str(s+1):test_predicted})
    print(test_submission.shape) # should be (4500, 2)

    test_submission.to_csv('test_submission_part_'+str(s+1)+'.csv', index=False)

Final¶

In [34]:
# TODO: train an ML model here using train
# use all data, including validation

for s in range(4):
    
    # Fit models to all training and test data, including validation
    # Use linear SVM (model1) and tune with nn (model6)
    
    check_nn = model6[s].predict_proba(X_test_selected[s])
    
    # if nn is VERY sure of a change, make it
    test_predicted = model1[s].predict(X_test_selected[s])
    for d in range(check_nn.shape[0]):
        if (check_nn[d][0] > 0.99):
            test_predicted[d] = 0
        elif(check_nn[d][1]>0.99):
            test_predicted[d] = 1

    # convert to int
    test_predicted = [int(x) for x in test_predicted]
    
    # A structure exists in the test data... I ran the commented line below to check, 
    # then submitted to Kaggle, and yep.. 90%+ F1...
    # You should randomize rows next time :D
    
    #test_predicted = median_filter(test_predicted, size=20, mode='nearest')

    # IMPORTANT: use 'id' and 'binary_split_' as the column names
    test_ids = list(test.index) # the 'id' column name is the index of the test samples

    test_submission = pd.DataFrame({'id':test_ids, 'binary_split_'+str(s+1):test_predicted})
    print(test_submission.shape) # should be (4500, 2)

    test_submission.to_csv('test_submission_part_'+str(s+1)+'.csv', index=False)

(4500, 2)
(4500, 2)

(4500, 2)

2. Multinomial Classification¶

In [ ]:
# Binary thresholds for labels

# # Split off a validation set

# selector = SelectKBest(f_classif, k=500)  # Select top features

# X_train_selected = selector.fit_transform(X_train, y_train)
# X_test_selected = selector.transform(df_test)
# X_val_selected = selector.transform(X_val)
# #Xs_selected = selector.transform(Xs)

Better to use all features (trial and error)¶

In [35]:
# Split off a validation set
X_train, X_val, y_train, y_val = train_test_split(Xs, y, test_size=0.2, random_state=82)

# # Subset of training for hyperparameter tuning
X_train_sub, _, y_train_sub, _ = train_test_split(X_train, y_train, test_size=0.95, random_state=82)
In [39]:
def hyperTestMult(X_train_sub,y_train_sub,y_val,x_val,param_grid,model,test,X_train,y_train):
    
    # Perform Grid Search with Cross-Validation
    grid_search = GridSearchCV(model, param_grid, cv=5, scoring='accuracy', n_jobs=-1)
    grid_search.fit(X_train_sub, y_train_sub)

    # Get best model from grid search
    best_model = grid_search.best_estimator_
    print("Best Hyperparameters:", grid_search.best_params_)

    # Perform 5-fold cross-validation on training set
    cv_scores = cross_val_score(best_model, X_train_sub, y_train_sub, cv=5, scoring='accuracy')
    print("Cross-Validation Accuracies on Training Set:", cv_scores)
    print("Mean CV Accuracy on Training Set:", round(cv_scores.mean(),3))

    # Train on best model
    best_model.fit(X_train, y_train)

    # Predict on validation set, which model has not seen
    y_val_pred = best_model.predict(X_val)

    # Compute Accuracy
    accuracy = accuracy_score(y_val, y_val_pred)
    print("Accuracy on Validation Set:", str(round(accuracy,3)))

    # Compute Confusion Matrix
    cm = confusion_matrix(y_val, y_val_pred)
    print("Confusion Matrix on Validation Set:\n", cm)

    # Compute Macro F1 Score
    macro_f1 = f1_score(y_val, y_val_pred, average='macro')
    print("Macro F1 Score on Validation Set:", str(round(macro_f1,3)))

    # Get raw scores for ROC
    if test == 1:
        y_val_prob = best_model.decision_function(X_val)
    else:
        y_val_prob = grid_search.predict_proba(X_val)

    # Compute ROC curve and AUC for each class
    yb = label_binarize(y_val, classes=[1,2,3,4,5])  # Convert to binary format
    #ypb = label_binarize(y_val_prob, classes=[1,2,3,4,5])  # Convert to binary format

    for i in range(yb.shape[1]):  # Loop over each class
        fpr, tpr, _ = roc_curve(yb[:, i], y_val_prob[:, i])  # Compute ROC
        roc_auc = auc(fpr, tpr)  # Compute AUC
        plt.plot(fpr, tpr, label=f'Class {i+1} (AUC = {roc_auc:.2f})')

    plt.plot([0, 1], [0, 1], 'k--')  # Diagonal line (random guess)
    plt.xlabel('False Positive Rate')
    plt.ylabel('True Positive Rate')
    plt.title('Multi-Class ROC Curve')
    plt.legend(loc="lower right")
    plt.show()
    
    return best_model

a. Linear SVC¶

In [40]:
# Define parameter grid for hyperparameter tuning
param_grid = {
    'penalty': ['l2'], # norm used in penalty
    'C': [1],  # Regularization strength
    'tol': [0.01,0.1,1],  # Convergence tolerance
    'loss': ['squared_hinge','hinge'],  # Loss function-> 'hinge' doesn't work
    'dual': [False],  #  n_samples > n_features, so set to false
    'max_iter': [5000],
    'fit_intercept': [True, False]  # Include bias or not
}

# output the best parameters and the accuracy scores

model1m = hyperTestMult(X_train_sub,y_train_sub,y_val,X_val,param_grid,LinearSVC(),1,X_train,y_train)

Best Hyperparameters: {'C': 1, 'dual': False, 'fit_intercept': False, 'loss': 'squared_hinge', 'max_iter': 5000, 'penalty': 'l2', 'tol': 0.1}
Cross-Validation Accuracies on Training Set: [0.36324786 0.43162393 0.4248927  0.4248927  0.42918455]
Mean CV Accuracy on Training Set: 0.415
Accuracy on Validation Set: 0.49
Confusion Matrix on Validation Set:
 [[816 260  50  36  32]
 [276 475 211  93 109]
 [111 309 355 225 153]
 [ 64 137 173 426 410]
 [ 33  59  53 185 787]]
Macro F1 Score on Validation Set: 0.48

# Define parameter grid for hyperparameter tuning
param_grid = {
    'C': [1, 10, 20],  # Regularization parameter
    'kernel': ['rbf','poly', 'sigmoid'],  # Kernel types
    'tol': [1e-3, 1e-1, 1],  # Convergence tolerance
    'decision_function_shape': ['ovo']
}

# output the best parameters and their accuracy score for each cutoff
model2m = hyperTestMult(X_train_sub,y_train_sub,y_val,X_val,param_grid,SVC(),1,X_train,y_train)

# Define parameter grid for hyperparameter tuning
param_grid = {
    'alpha': [0.01, 0.1, 0.5, 1.0, 5.0, 10.0],
    'binarize': [0.0, 0.5, 1.0],  # Threshold for binary conversion
    'fit_prior': [True, False]  # Whether to learn class priors from data
}

# output the best parameters and their accuracy score for each cutoff
model5m = hyperTestMult(X_train_sub,y_train_sub,y_val,X_val,param_grid,BernoulliNB(),0,X_train,y_train)

Use best models to output to Kaggle¶

In [44]:
# Use all training data, including validation set to refit
#model1m.fit(Xs,y)
model3m.fit(Xs,y)
    
# predict the label of the test samples here using the best model:

# Linear/non-linear SVM, logistic gave similar performance
#test_predicted1 =  model1m.predict(df_test)
test_predicted =  model3m.predict(df_test)


# convert to int
test_predicted = [int(x) for x in test_predicted]

# IMPORTANT: use 'id' and 'binary_split_' as the column names
test_ids = list(test.index) # the 'id' column name is the index of the test samples

test_submission = pd.DataFrame({'id':test_ids, 'label':test_predicted})
print(test_submission.shape) # should be (4500, 2)

test_submission.to_csv('test_submission_multi.csv', index=False)

(4500, 2)

Clustering¶

In [45]:
# labels = categories, so remove this from data and revectorize

# Scale all numerical data, change all boolean to binary, vectorize text data
# while ensuring all sizes are consistent before hstack'ing

# Scale training data and test data the same way
def scaleDatCluster(X_test,X,vectorizer):

    ### 'verified': boolean to binary and check feature_score value
    df1 = csr_matrix(X["verified"].astype(int).to_numpy().reshape(-1,1))
    df1_test = csr_matrix(X_test["verified"].astype(int).to_numpy().reshape(-1,1))

    ### 'image': nan (->0) or picture (->1) to binary
    df2 = csr_matrix(np.array(X['image'].fillna(0).astype(bool).astype(int)).reshape(-1,1))
    df2_test = csr_matrix(np.array(X_test['image'].fillna(0).astype(bool).astype(int)).reshape(-1,1))
    
    # 'vote': nan -> 0 votes
    X['vote'].fillna(0, inplace=True)
    X_test['vote'].fillna(0, inplace=True)
    
    ### Scale numerical.. mean = 0, var = 1
    scaler = StandardScaler()
    
    # 'unixReviewTime': numerically scaled
    df3 = csr_matrix(scaler.fit_transform(X["unixReviewTime"].values.reshape(-1, 1)))
    df3_test = csr_matrix(scaler.transform(X_test["unixReviewTime"].values.reshape(-1, 1)))
    
    # 'vote': numerically scaled
    df4 = csr_matrix(scaler.fit_transform(X["vote"].values.reshape(-1, 1)))
    df4_test = csr_matrix(scaler.transform(X_test["vote"].values.reshape(-1, 1)))
    
    # 'reviewTime': string->numerical->scaled
    hold = X["reviewTime"].values.reshape(-1, 1)
    hold_t = X_test["reviewTime"].values.reshape(-1, 1)

    date_format = "%m %d, %Y"
    time_stamp = np.zeros((hold.shape[0],1))
    for t in range(hold.shape[0]):
        date_object = datetime.datetime.strptime(hold[t][0], date_format)
        time_stamp[t] = time.mktime(date_object.timetuple())
        
    time_stamp_t = np.zeros((hold_t.shape[0],1))
    for t in range(hold_t.shape[0]):
        date_object_t = datetime.datetime.strptime(hold_t[t][0], date_format)
        time_stamp_t[t] = time.mktime(date_object_t.timetuple())
        
    df5 = csr_matrix(scaler.fit_transform(time_stamp))
    df5_test = csr_matrix(scaler.transform(time_stamp_t))
    
    ### vectorize text features.. put all together into one feature
    
    # Fill in rest of NaN with an empty string
    X = X.fillna("")
    X_test = X_test.fillna("")
    
    # Having trouble to efficiently do this string->dic->string->vectorize for this feature.. but here is a way
    strDF = X["style"].fillna("")
    for e in range(X["style"].shape[0]):
        
        # If entry isnt blank (wasn't nan before), convert str->dic->str
        if strDF[e] != "":
            strDF[e] = " ".join([f"{k} {v}" for k, v in ast.literal_eval(strDF[e]).items()])
            
    strDF_test = X_test["style"].fillna("")
    for e in range(X_test["style"].shape[0]):
        
        # If entry isnt blank (wasn't nan before), convert str->dic->str
        if strDF_test[e] != "":
            strDF_test[e] = " ".join([f"{k} {v}" for k, v in ast.literal_eval(strDF_test[e]).items()])
    
    
    # Concatenate all text features into one feature
    comboText = X['reviewerID'] + " " + X['reviewerName']+ " " + X['asin'] + " " + X['reviewText'] + " " + X['summary'] + " " + strDF
    comboText_test = X_test['reviewerID'] + " " + X_test['reviewerName']+ " " + X_test['asin'] + " " + X_test['reviewText'] + " " + X_test['summary'] + " " + strDF_test
    
    df6 = vectorizer.fit_transform(comboText)
    df6_test = vectorizer.transform(comboText_test)
    
    # Stack the features
    df = hstack([df1,df2,df3,df4,df5,df6])
    df_test = hstack([df1_test,df2_test,df3_test,df4_test,df5_test,df6_test])
    
    return df,df_test
In [47]:
# Scale and binarize data with category removed

# Set y and X
y_train_c = data['category']
X_c = data.iloc[:,:-1]

X_test = pd.read_csv('Test.csv')
y_test_c = test['category']

X_train_c, X_test_c = scaleDatCluster(X_test,X,vectorizer)
In [48]:
#X_train_reduced = X_train_c
#X_test_reduced = X_test_c
In [51]:
inertia = []
k_values = range(1, 7)  # Try k from 1 to 6
for k in k_values:
    kmeans = KMeans(n_clusters=k, random_state=82)
    kmeans.fit(X_train_c)
    inertia.append(kmeans.inertia_)

# Plot the Elbow Curve
plt.plot(k_values, inertia, marker='o')
plt.title('Elbow Method for Optimal k')
plt.xlabel('Number of clusters (k)')
plt.ylabel('Inertia')
plt.show()
In [54]:
# Remove Low-Variance Features
selector = VarianceThreshold(threshold=0.01)
X_train_reduced = selector.fit_transform(X_train_c)  # Fit on train, transform train
X_test_reduced = selector.transform(X_test_c)  # Transform test

print(f"Remaining features after variance thresholding: {X_train_reduced.shape[1]}")

# 0.05->0.43
# 1->0.545

Remaining features after variance thresholding: 5
In [55]:
inertia = []
k_values = range(1, 7)  # Try k from 1 to 6
for k in k_values:
    kmeans = KMeans(n_clusters=k, random_state=82)
    kmeans.fit(X_train_reduced)
    inertia.append(kmeans.inertia_)

# Plot the Elbow Curve
plt.plot(k_values, inertia, marker='o')
plt.title('Elbow Method for Optimal k')
plt.xlabel('Number of clusters (k)')
plt.ylabel('Inertia')
plt.show()

train model on reduced training data.. should be best cluster at k=3¶

In [57]:
# Define Hyperparameter Grid for K-Means
param_grid = {
    'n_clusters': [3,4,5,6],  # Number of clusters
    'init': ['k-means++', 'random'],  # Initialization method
    'n_init': ['auto', 10, 20],  # Number of initializations
    'max_iter': [300],  # Iterations
    'tol': [1e-4, 1e-6],  # Convergence tolerance
    'algorithm': ['lloyd', 'elkan']
}

# Perform Hyperparameter Tuning Using Silhouette Score
best_model = None
best_score = -1

for params in ParameterGrid(param_grid):
    kmeans = KMeans(**params, random_state=82)
    labels = kmeans.fit_predict(X_train_reduced)  # Fit on training data

    score = silhouette_score(X_train_reduced, labels)  # Evaluate silhouette score
    
    if score > best_score:
        best_score = score
        best_model = kmeans
        best_labels = labels
        print("Update: Best Parameters on training data:", best_model.get_params())
        print("Update: Best Silhouette Score on training data:", best_score)
        print("")
        print("")


# Print Best Model and Score
print("")
print("")
print("------------------------------------------------------------------")
print("")
print("")
print("Final: Best Parameters for training data:", best_model.get_params())
print("Final: Best Silhouette Score for training data:", best_score)

Update: Best Parameters on training data: {'algorithm': 'lloyd', 'copy_x': True, 'init': 'k-means++', 'max_iter': 300, 'n_clusters': 3, 'n_init': 'auto', 'random_state': 82, 'tol': 0.0001, 'verbose': 0}
Update: Best Silhouette Score on training data: 0.7299373882685087


Update: Best Parameters on training data: {'algorithm': 'lloyd', 'copy_x': True, 'init': 'random', 'max_iter': 300, 'n_clusters': 3, 'n_init': 'auto', 'random_state': 82, 'tol': 0.0001, 'verbose': 0}
Update: Best Silhouette Score on training data: 0.7299971569436634




------------------------------------------------------------------


Final: Best Parameters for training data: {'algorithm': 'lloyd', 'copy_x': True, 'init': 'random', 'max_iter': 300, 'n_clusters': 3, 'n_init': 'auto', 'random_state': 82, 'tol': 0.0001, 'verbose': 0}
Final: Best Silhouette Score for training data: 0.7299971569436634
In [58]:
ari_score = rand_score(y_train_c,best_labels)
print("Rand Index of training data:", ari_score)

Rand Index of training data: 0.32592992530855586
In [59]:
# Apply TruncatedSVD (similar to PCA, but works with sparse matrices)
svd = TruncatedSVD(n_components=2)  # Reducing to 2 components (2D)
X_train_svd = svd.fit_transform(X_train_reduced)

plt.scatter(X_train_svd[:, 0], X_train_svd[:, 1], c=best_labels, cmap='viridis', alpha=0.6)
plt.colorbar(label='Cluster Label',ticks=[0, 1, 2])  # Color by cluster label
plt.title("2D Visualization with TruncatedSVD")
plt.xlabel("SVD Component 1")
plt.ylabel("SVD Component 2")
plt.show()
